description: Breeding / crossbreeding resolution data. From breeding.ts and RULES-SPEC.md section 9. Resolution is a coin flip per stat row against the two parent Species, with exceptions applied BEFORE the relevant flip. Includes the function/sensitivity reverse cause-index authored per decision D9 (option 1, build the reverse index).

cadence.replicationNote: The same hybrid may be replicated (a new Colony of the SAME Species) on subsequent dawns until a new hybrid roll is made, at which point old replicate stats are lost. A replicate creates no new Species and no new registry entry.

statRowNotes.resolveFungalTypeFirst: fungalType (row 1) resolves first; type-specific dimension rows only apply to the resolved type (D20).
statRowNotes.typeMismatchReroll: If a coin flip would inherit a dimension from a parent of a different type than the resolved offspring, re-roll that dimension from the resolved type's own base formula in types.json (D20) — the same no-crossover logic as moldRtfmNoCrossover.
statRowNotes.traditionalCapRadius: derived: volva radius added, per the resolved parent's volva
statRowNotes.shelfCapDiameter: Per D11 (generation-tables governs): shelf cap diameter = 2d10 from types.json; the breeding-sheet 'half of traditional cap radius' note is treated as stale. Shelf cap height = capDiameter / 2 (round up).
statRowNotes.shelfCapDiameterConflict: D11 is flagged OPEN; if the owner chooses the breeding-sheet derivation instead, change this row's resolution to derive from the traditional parent's cap radius.

mushroomAlwaysEdible.rule: Mushrooms can never carry a non-edible function regardless of what a coin flip would assign. Checked when resolvedFungalType === 'mushroom'.

moldRtfmNoCrossover.rule: Mold's RTFM formula never crosses to a non-mold offspring or vice versa. If the flip would assign a mold parent's rtfmDays to a non-mold result (or the reverse), re-roll RTFM from the resolved fungalType's own base formula in types.json.

poisonTieBreakNotStack.rule: If BOTH parents have the poison function, effects do not stack: coin-flip which single parent's poison effect is inherited.

sporeVirilityTieBreakNotStack.rule: Same non-stacking tie-break as poison, for spore virility.

functionSensitivityInheritedAsRerollUnle.rule: Functions and sensitivities are NOT inherited as a package with their triggering condition. If both parents display the SAME function/sensitivity it carries over directly. If they differ (or only one parent has it), the offspring's function/sensitivity is re-rolled at random from the union of physical-trait sets that could have caused either parent's version (see reverseCauseIndex).

flavorsAndFunctionsMayStack.rule: Flavors and functions may each stack from both parents (independently rolled per row) — the default case, listed for completeness against the non-stacking exceptions above.

reverseCauseIndex.description: D9 deliverable. Maps a function category / sensitivity outcome back to the physical-trait sets that could have caused it, so the reroll exception (functionSensitivityInheritedAsRerollUnlessMatching) can pick uniformly from the union of both parents' causal sets. 'functionRoll' entries mean the outcome can also arise from the base d10 function table (functions.json), so a reroll may instead re-roll that table for the resolved type.

sensitivities.note: A sensitivity's causal trait set is exactly its trigger predicate in sensitivities.json. When rerolling a sensitivity, draw uniformly from the union of both parents' triggering rule ids; the chosen rule's trigger defines the physical traits to reconstruct. No separate table is needed here — sensitivities.json is the index.
